title: Business ideation and validation studio
scenario: Build a business ideation studio that uses Feynman loops, falsification, counter-evidence, experiment design, scoring, decision journals, Karpathy retention, skill thresholds, and critic review.
termination: Stop only when the idea has falsifiable assumptions, counter-evidence, experiment plan, scoring rubric, decision journal, critic review, and retained lessons; generated enthusiasm is not evidence.

prompts.prerequisites: Verify target market, customer/job hypothesis, evidence-quality rubric, source policy, experiment budget, and decision owner.
prompts.discovery: Read existing product notes, market constraints, research sources, Feynman loop guide, Karpathy retention rules, and any business-domain source material.
prompts.feynman: Explain the customer, job-to-be-done, painful alternative, adoption trigger, buyer, channel, and evidence needed to disprove the idea.
prompts.kbd_assess: Assess assumptions, target users, risks, counter-evidence, experiment budget, success thresholds, and decision timing.
prompts.kbd_analyze: Analyze market sources, comparable products, contrary evidence, user segments, pricing constraints, distribution channels, and legal/ethical constraints.
prompts.kbd_spec: Specify assumption ledger, falsification tests, experiment cards, scoring rubric, decision journal, research source registry, and retention policy.
prompts.kbd_plan: Plan learning work before build work: Feynman gaps, research, interview/smoke tests, prototype threshold, critic review, and retention.
prompts.research: Use primary sources where possible, label vendor/marketing claims, seek disconfirming evidence first, and record access dates.
prompts.implementation: Build only the minimum prototype or research artifact needed to test the highest-risk assumption. Do not expand product scope until the evidence threshold is met.
prompts.public_boundary_verification: Run the selected experiment, collect evidence, score the result, update the decision journal, and show what decision the evidence supports.
prompts.independent_critic: Challenge the idea, evidence quality, scoring, market assumptions, customer specificity, and whether the next build step follows from evidence.
prompts.reflection_retention: Record assumptions disproven, prompt improvements, new skill thresholds, decision rationale, and private context boundaries in Karpathy memory.
prompts.recovery: If research becomes confirmatory or vague, rerun the Feynman loop with a falsification rubric and independent critic.
prompts.stop_conditions: Stop for no falsifiable assumption, no counter-evidence, unsupported market claims, unclear buyer, missing scoring rubric, or evidence that fails the build threshold.
